---
title: "Loan Approval Analysis"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: cosmo
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

loan_data <- read.csv("loan_data_clean.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    loan_approved = as.logical(loan_approved),
    credit_category = factor(credit_category, levels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good", "Excellent")),
    employment_level = factor(employment_level, levels = c("Entry (0-10 yrs)", "Mid (11-25 yrs)", "Senior (26+ yrs)")),
    debt_burden_category = factor(debt_burden_category, levels = c("Low (<20%)", "Moderate (20-40%)", "High (≥40%)"))
  )

colors <- c("Poor" = "#d32f2f", "Fair" = "#ff9800", "Good" = "#9ccc65", "Excellent" = "#388e3c")
```

Row {data-height=90}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Total
```{r}
valueBox("2,000", caption = "Applications", icon = "fa-users", color = "#607D8B")
```

### Approved
```{r}
valueBox("44%", caption = "879 Approved", icon = "fa-check", color = "#4CAF50")
```

### High-Value
```{r}
valueBox("405", caption = "$40k+ Loans (20%)", icon = "fa-dollar-sign", color = "#FF9800")
```

### Mean Score
```{r}
valueBox("574", caption = "Avg Credit Score", icon = "fa-line-chart", color = "#2196F3")
```

### Critical
```{r}
valueBox("0/195", caption = "Poor Credit ($40k+)", icon = "fa-warning", color = "#F44336")
```

Row {data-height=455}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Credit Score Drives Approval
```{r fig.height=5.5}
q1 <- loan_data %>%
  group_by(credit_category, employment_level) %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(loan_approved) * 100, .groups = "drop")

ggplot(q1, aes(employment_level, credit_category, fill = rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(rate), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 10, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#d32f2f", mid = "#ff9800", high = "#388e3c",
                       midpoint = 50, limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
```

### Debt Burden Impact
```{r fig.height=5.5}
q3 <- loan_data %>%
  group_by(debt_burden_category, credit_category) %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(loan_approved) * 100, .groups = "drop")

ggplot(q3, aes(debt_burden_category, rate, color = credit_category, group = credit_category)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 3.5) +
  geom_point(size = 8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(rate), "%")), 
            vjust = -1.5, size = 5.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#212121", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 120), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Approval %") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
```

### High-Value Loans ($40k+): Best Combinations
```{r fig.height=5.5}
q2 <- loan_data %>% 
  filter(high_loan) %>%
  group_by(credit_category, employment_level) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    rate = mean(loan_approved) * 100, 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n >= 5) %>%
  arrange(desc(rate)) %>%
  mutate(combo = paste(credit_category, employment_level, sep = " + "))

ggplot(q2, aes(reorder(combo, rate), rate, fill = credit_category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(rate), "%")), 
            hjust = 1.1, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Approval %") +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 25, 20, 20))
```

Row {data-height=455}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Application Volume {data-width=600}
```{r fig.height=5.5}
vol <- loan_data %>%
  group_by(credit_category) %>%
  summarise(total = n(), approved = sum(loan_approved), 
            rate = mean(loan_approved) * 100, .groups = "drop")

ggplot(vol, aes(credit_category, total, fill = credit_category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_col(aes(y = approved), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(y = total, label = total), 
            vjust = -0.6, size = 6, fontface = "bold", color = "#212121") +
  geom_text(aes(y = approved, label = paste0(approved, "\n(", round(rate), "%)")), 
            vjust = 1.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Applications") +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
```

### Key Metrics {data-width=400}
```{r}
metrics <- data.frame(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  Category = c("**CREDIT SCORE**", "Poor (300-579)", "Fair (580-669)", 
               "Good (670-739)", "Excellent (740+)", "",
               "**EMPLOYMENT**", "Entry (0-10y)", "Mid (11-25y)", "Senior (26+y)", "",
               "**DEBT BURDEN**", "Low (<20%)", "Moderate (20-40%)", "High (≥40%)", "",
               "**HIGH-VALUE ($40k+)**", "Total apps", "Poor credit", "Excellent credit"),
  Count = c("", "1,008", "322", "255", "415", "",
            "", "527", "735", "738", "",
            "", "691", "739", "570", "",
            "", "405", "195", "80"),
  Rate = c("", "7.8%", "56.5%", "82.4%", "98.3%", "",
           "", "33.0%", "47.9%", "47.8%", "",
           "", "57.9%", "42.6%", "28.8%", "",
           "", "39.3%", "0%", "98.8%")
)

knitr::kable(metrics, col.names = c("", "Apps", "Approval"), align = c("l", "r", "r")) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), 
                           full_width = TRUE, font_size = 14, position = "left") %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = "#2196F3") %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(c(1, 7, 12, 17), bold = TRUE, background = "#E8F5E9", font_size = 15)
```
